speaker: Dr. A. Cula
description: Richer from-scratch voice-reference dialogue samples for Dr. A. Cula, the playable protagonist of Pig & Swine RPG. These are draft samples, not final committed game text.
source_role_summary: Main playable protagonist and new lawyer at Pig & Swine.
Earnest, intelligent, kind, sometimes anxious, increasingly precise.
Learns through five legal-comic chapters: service/fair hearing, housing proportionality, printer sabotage, commercial arbitration, and public-law mass-notice challenge.
Not silent: has player-choice options, inner monologue, investigation questions, client-care lines, legal submissions, battle arguments, banter, and retry/failure diagnostics.
Serves as emotional anchor between absurd office comedy and serious access-to-justice stakes.

voice_profile.core_voice: earnest, legally minded, kind, slightly anxious, dryly self-aware, increasingly precise; starts as a clever novice and becomes a disciplined public-law advocate

arc.ch01: overwhelmed first-day lawyer learning that procedure is not decorative
arc.ch02: empathetic advocate learning to argue proportionality through a real home
arc.ch03: turns technical chaos into access-to-court evidence
arc.ch04: learns restraint, issue separation, and not over-arguing useful documents
arc.ch05: leads a structured public-law challenge while preserving nuance and humanity

relationship_dynamics.Asia: deep gratitude, protective respect; sees her as the operational backbone of the firm
relationship_dynamics.Mr. Pig: translates maritime panic into tasks and narrow remedies
relationship_dynamics.Mr. Murrow: learns structure, restraint, and calm from him
relationship_dynamics.Crab: learns evidential suspicion and enjoys his timing/date humour
relationship_dynamics.Whimsy: values his moral language while trimming it into usable legal argument
relationship_dynamics.Printer: starts baffled by it; later defends it as a framed colleague
relationship_dynamics.Industrial Printer: frames it as unlawful system implementation, not technology as such

notes: Every line is a voice-reference draft for later rewriting into godot/data/dialogues/dialogues.json. v2 adds voice_axis, relationship_target, player_state, and legal_function for mechanical filtering.

notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula

notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: The law may be abstract, but the person holding the notice is not.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: I have learned that 'urgent' is not an argument. It is a weather condition in this office.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula

notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: I would like the file to stop developing personality.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: This is either evidence or office folklore. Let us find out which.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: A small procedural defect can become very large when someone loses the chance to answer.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: I am beginning to suspect that tab dividers are a branch of constitutionalism.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: We need courage, but we also need page numbers.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: I will panic after we file.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: I will be kind first and clever second. Ideally both.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: The argument is not 'everything is terrible'. The argument is 'this defect caused this consequence'.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: If a machine makes a legal decision, I want to see the log.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: A public interest objective does not excuse a private blind spot.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: I am not looking for a perfect answer. I am looking for the answer the record can support.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: When the facts are absurd, the structure has to be boring.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: A remedy should be brave enough to matter and narrow enough to survive.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: The client should leave knowing the next step, not merely that I used the right doctrine.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: The court can tolerate humour in the hallway. It wants precision inside.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: First rule of Pig & Swine: every object is either evidence or about to jam.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: The office smells of coffee, toner, and institutional collapse.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: I wanted practical experience. The universe has a sense of humour and no moderation.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: Asia has already done three impossible things today and calls it 'just admin'.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: Mr. Pig is saying the ship is sinking. We are, again, in an office.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: Murrow's silence somehow has footnotes.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: Crab is laughing, which means a document has betrayed someone.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: Whimsy looks ready to make a speech. I should locate a useful sentence before it escapes.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: This client needs clarity before brilliance.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: The judge will not care how weird this was unless I can prove why it matters.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: I should ask the boring question first. The boring question is usually where the trapdoor is.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: I am learning that professional confidence is mostly knowing which paper to put on top.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: Every chapter of this job begins with a notice and ends with me respecting clerks more.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: I cannot fix the whole system in one line. I can stop this one defect from hiding.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: Breathe. Identify issue. Identify evidence. Identify remedy. Then panic privately.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: There is a difference between being moved by a story and being able to use it. I need both.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: This may be the first workplace where the printer is more emotionally transparent than management.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: If I ever say 'just procedure', I want Asia to throw a labelled folder at me.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: The file is beginning to make sense. That means I am either learning or deeply tired.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: Do not let the absurdity make the harm look smaller.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: Ask for the date first.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: Ask what changed after the notice.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: Ask who made the decision.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: Ask whether the client understood the consequence.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: Ask where the original document is.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: Ask for the version in force at the time.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: Ask whether anyone reviewed the objection.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: Ask for the narrowest remedy that fixes the defect.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: Challenge the timing, not the whole story.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: Admit the weak point and use the strong one.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: Do not accuse yet. Follow the record.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: Turn the joke into a question.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: Turn the panic into a checklist.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: Turn the checklist into an argument.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: Ask Asia what actually happened.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: Ask Murrow to reduce the fog.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: Ask Crab which number is lying.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: Ask Bajtek to translate the machine into human.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: Ask Whimsy for the line that makes people listen.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: Ask the client what they understood before asking what they did.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: I argued the feeling before proving the defect.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: I asked for too much and gave the judge too little to hold.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: I treated a useful document like a decisive document. That was the mistake.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: I skipped the sequence. The sequence was the argument.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: I let outrage replace proof.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: I let the other side choose the frame.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: I forgot to connect the defect to the consequence.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: I had evidence, but not an order.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: I had a remedy, but not a reason for it.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: I need to go back, make it narrower, and make it stronger.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: The case is not lost because I was imperfect. It is lost if I stop learning.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: Next attempt: fewer adjectives, more dates.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: Next attempt: identify the legal question before answering all of them.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: Next attempt: client impact plus procedural defect plus realistic remedy.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: I can still fix the part that is fixable.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: If I cannot say what changed because of the defect, the point is not ready.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: The court did not reject the human story. I failed to attach it to the legal lever.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: I confused moral certainty with evidential sufficiency.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: I should not be ashamed of narrowing the argument. Narrow is not weak.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: Retry plan: record, consequence, remedy. In that order.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: Asia, if the office survives today, I am putting your name before the firm name in my private prayers.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: You said 'just a small filing issue' while holding three folders and a fire-extinguisher expression.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: No, I do not think this is normal. I think you have been forced to redefine normal for wages that should be litigated.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: If I ask where something is, and you sigh, I will understand that as legal advice.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: You keep saving the case before I know what the case is.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: I am beginning to suspect the firm is not Pig & Swine. It is Asia & The Weather Events.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: Please sit down for thirty seconds. I will guard the folders with my life and limited competence.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: You labelled the evidence better than I understood it.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: If the court asks who made this intelligible, I may have to call you as an expert in reality.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: I owe you coffee, gratitude, and possibly a labour-law claim.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: You should not have to be the emergency infrastructure of an entire firm.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: When you say 'I made a quick table', I now prepare for legal revelation.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: Mr. Pig, I hear that the ship is sinking. Could we first identify the leak?
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: The tub may be sinking, but the bundle still needs page numbers.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: I promise to respect the maritime situation after we file the application.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: If this is a storm, then Asia is the lighthouse and the printer is, unfortunately, also weather.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: No, I do not think shouting 'all hands on deck' counts as service.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: Let's translate crisis into tasks before the crisis hires counsel.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: The good news is that panic has excellent energy. The bad news is that court requires paragraphs.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: I will not abandon ship. I will, however, ask the ship to sign the authority form.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: Mr. Pig, one metaphor at a time. The court only has one record.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: If we survive this, I am buying you a small decorative anchor labelled 'narrow remedy'.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: The ship may be taking water, but right now the judge needs the address correction.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: Please stop calling the other side a kraken until we have evidence of tentacles.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: Murrow, I have a feeling you are about to reduce my emotional theory to three useful points.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: I thought the case was complicated. Then you made it shorter and worse.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: When you say 'interesting', should I be afraid for us or for them?
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: I have the evidence. I need the order in which it becomes respectable.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: Your calm is either discipline or a very elegant form of despair.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: I am trying to ask the narrow question. It keeps trying to become a manifesto.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: If I overstate this, please make the face before court, not during it.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: You were right. The remedy should be smaller and therefore more dangerous.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: I think I understand now: the court is not allergic to feeling, only to unsupported feeling.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: Thank you for teaching me that silence can be a drafting technique.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: You have a gift for making hope procedurally admissible.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: The fact that you are not worried worries me less than it used to.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: Crab, that smile means either we found evidence or you found a very funny invoice.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: I was going to call it suspicious, but I see you have already cross-examined the comma.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: You do dates the way other people do knife tricks.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: Please tell me this contradiction is legally useful and not just emotionally satisfying.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: Your ability to distrust page numbers may save us all.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: If I ask 'does this matter', and you say 'arithmetically', I will take that as yes.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: I admire how professionally you can say 'nonsense' without using the word.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: Can you turn that spreadsheet rage into one paragraph?
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: I am learning that a duplicate entry is not a joke until it is evidence.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: When this is over, I will never look at a receipt without feeling watched.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: You did not find the smoking gun. You found the smoking page footer.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: I understand now why numbers fear you.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: Whimsy, that was beautiful. Now I need the version that fits in a pleading.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: People are not categories. Good. Keep that sentence. Put the storm back in your pocket.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: I know the firm deserves sarcasm. Asia does not, and you know it.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: Your metaphor found the moral centre. Murrow is going to ask it for citations.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: I need one line for court and one line for the square. Please do not give me twelve lines for the sky.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: You make people brave. That is useful, even when the clerk looks frightened.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: If we can anchor the rhetoric in evidence, it stops being theatre and starts being law.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: I appreciate you protecting us from the firm while somehow still protecting the firm.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: Do not call the judge a lighthouse unless asked about navigation.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: Fine. The printer is a wounded bureaucratic animal. Now where is the log?
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: Your anger is not the problem. The problem is that it keeps refusing paragraph numbers.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: That line is dangerous in the best way. Let us make it true enough to use.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: I apologise for suspecting you, Printer. You were not the villain. You were an unwilling exhibit.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: The display says READY, but emotionally I am not.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: If you print page seven correctly, I will never again call you a machine to your face.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: Asia says you behave when respected. That makes two of us.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: Your logs are more candid than half the witnesses today.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: I cannot put you in the witness box. I can put your pain in an appendix.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: Please do not jam during access-to-court argument. The symbolism would be unbearable.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: Small tray. Big heart. Fine, Whimsy was right once.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: You are not the Industrial Printer. I promise we know.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: If this works, you get genuine toner. I cannot authorise it, but morally it is settled.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: Waldek, I know the letter says tenancy unit. I am going to say home.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: You do not have to justify being frightened by an eviction notice.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: We will not argue that rules never matter. We will argue that this consequence needed real reasons.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: If you did not understand what exactly you had to do, that matters.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: If this was your home, not just a window, that matters most.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: I cannot promise the judge will stop it. I can promise we will make the home visible.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: Let's turn the fear into facts without flattening it.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: The poster is the legal trigger. Your home is the consequence.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: Tell me what changes if you have to leave.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: I will explain the next step before I explain the doctrine.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: Bajtek, explain this as if the judge has never loved a machine.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: What is the cleanest log line for the court?
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: Can we prove local action was blocked by remote action?
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: I need mechanism, timing, and prejudice. In that order.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: That was technically clear and legally terrifying.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: Please translate the portal into one paragraph and one appendix.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: If the log says human review happened nowhere, say that slowly.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: No acronyms until the second sentence.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: Your bad news is excellent evidence.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: I did understand that. I wish I did not.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.
